message:
fix(core): eliminate async lock guard hazards, Mutex poison panics, and TcpStream leaks

Addresses three concurrency safety epics:

1. Async lock guards held across await points (ft-2d4gk):
   - runtime.rs: Split storage lock into separate acquisitions per await
   - workflows.rs: Document intentional tokio::sync::Mutex usage with
     safety rationale (no re-entrant locking, atomic policy+send)
   - notifications.rs: Change mute_store from Mutex to RwLock since
     is_event_muted() only needs &self (read access)

2. Mutex::lock().unwrap() poison handling (ft-ovnmj):
   - Replace 48 production .lock().unwrap()/.expect() calls with
     .lock().unwrap_or_else(PoisonError::into_inner) across 6 files:
     recorder_audit.rs, recorder_query.rs, sequence_model.rs,
     work_stealing_deque.rs, restore_scrollback.rs, replay_capture.rs

3. TcpStream shutdown enforcement (ft-1lmbf):
   - metrics.rs: Add explicit socket.shutdown().await before return
   - web.rs: Add explicit stream.shutdown(Both) in poke_listener()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
